comments.author: @adriaanvangennep8998
comments.text:
Everything beautiful there, sometimes not comparable and understandable, just because all is different as anyone think it is ! The historical achieved goals last 40 years were at excellent levels in quality and quantities. But different, and in the eyes of many Europeans and Americans surely too, not according to reallity. Definitions about quality of life are difficult to explain. The impact from history and Soviet times has surely impacted the civil societies inside Russia too. But one thing is very clear, Russian culture is very near in the social sense of the word to Europeans, and not Americans so much. In fact the differences between Europe and America has a long bond, but the culture there was changing every day since the establishment 250-300 years ago in a similar way European seeing Russia today. All for the same reasons. The old story about East-West cold-war period is debet here, because it was and still is never resolved, even today. Political power and absolute hegemony of USD, economy and militairy power made what America is today. A great nation, but with problems Europe not had, but sometimes just sucked into it.
Emeriging global acting economic powers made America paranoia which is logic, it has a lot to loose, but has answers to correct surely too with two-way debates instead one-way steamroller policies. The same for the Russians, global competition with Russia last 100 years was dictated by Americal alone. Global cooperation is the ultimate answer to resolve problems as any problem has three fast solid solutions and at seen which need debates about adaption to reallity. Not doing so shall ignite new conlficts and as we see today wars for the wrong reasons. The world has a bright future with all technical achievenments and sciences but need a clear adaption to reallity too in the economic-financial fields slashing bureaucracy and keeping prices as low as possible. But here appears the largest human mistake, human created economic-financial BS, and double standards and therefore without accountability the world dont need to succeed ! 😎💪🎉🎉🎉

comments.author: @monmixer
comments.text: Plumbing is exposed in buildings in Russia so it's easy to repair. Boilers and hot water heat most apt buildings. Hot pipes and registers make heat. A theatre I work in here in the USA actually has water line heat and registers.

replies.author: @enryo3043
replies.text: ​@CountryDocumentary8386 depends on the skill of your foreman and his installation crew, and which products you use.

replies.author: @ponzo1967
replies.text: As if utilities are something unsightly and shameful lol Then Americans complain when a repair costs $800 because the wall has to be removed to fix it.

replies.author: @enryo3043
replies.text: Repairability is best.

replies.author: @CountryDocumentary8386
replies.text: That actually makes a lot of sense. Do you think exposed plumbing is more practical, or does it make buildings look unfinished to most people?

comments.author: @KOS762
comments.text: Russia is like Canada, they have all that land, because no one else wants it.  Russia has 3-4 major population centers, and the rest is dirt roads and outhouses.  They are the size of Pluto but have less modernized cities than Cuba.  Like I said 3-4 MAX.

replies.author: @CountryDocumentary8386
replies.text: @KOS762 Exactly, fear becomes part of the education. But that makes me wonder: if a whole society is trained to stay silent, what finally breaks that silence truth, suffering, or a new generation with nothing left to lose?

replies.author: @CountryDocumentary8386
replies.text: @KOS762 Exactly, fear becomes part of the education. But that makes me wonder: if a whole society is trained to stay silent, what finally breaks that silence truth, suffering, or a new generation with nothing left to lose?

replies.author: @KOS762
replies.text: @CountryDocumentary8386 They will be taught to not protest or face criminal charges.

replies.author: @CountryDocumentary8386
replies.text: @KOS762 That’s the hardest part: truth is not just about access to information, it’s about whether people are ready to accept it. Do you think Russia changes when the older generation fades, or does the system simply teach the next generation to stay quiet?

replies.author: @KOS762
replies.text: @CountryDocumentary8386 I think its is both.  The old generation believe state TV, no matter what anyone says.   Then the younger generation are using internet and tech in general, and see the truths and faults.  So you have a younger group trying to convince a older group things are wrong.  The conflict is with themselves.   Sure fear has a lot to do with it too.   If they could afford to leave, they already have... more or less.  Many just don't know there is even a war.  Bottom line is the Russian people are also lied too.  The Question is, will they ever know the truth, and if they did hear it, would they believe it?

replies.author: @CountryDocumentary8386
replies.text: @KOS762 That’s a powerful point. Maybe Russia’s biggest weapon isn’t land or resources, but control over the story. But do you think ordinary Russians truly believe it, or are many just afraid to question it?

replies.author: @KOS762
replies.text: @CountryDocumentary8386 I think Russia's strength goes into the fact that the people have no idea what is going on most of the time.  Russia attacks Ukraine for 10 years straight, and the Russian people have no idea why Ukraine is attacking them.   The strength is the lie.  Russian people go to war because they are told to, but don't understand why, or at least don't know the truth.

replies.author: @CountryDocumentary8386
replies.text: @KOS762 Exactly, that’s why Russia’s size is both a shield and a burden. But if most of the land is nearly empty, do you think Russia’s real strength is territory, resources, or just the few cities that actually matter?

replies.author: @KOS762
replies.text: @CountryDocumentary8386 It would make it harder to take over, considering the distances.  Weaker because Russia would be spread very thin, to defend all that area.  SO it could go either way, depending on what Russia wants to defend.   Cold and frozen tundra is mostly empty, just like Canada's Yukon.  Those areas would take a ton of resources to attack or defend.

replies.author: @CountryDocumentary8386
replies.text: Interesting take, but isn’t that true for many huge countries? The real question is: does Russia’s empty land make it weak, or does it give it hidden power?
